summary:
Despite shared early symptoms like fever and cough,COVID-19 can often be distinguished from influenza clinicaUy by the presence of anosmia or dysgeusia, which are far less common in flu. Gastrointestinal symptoms also provide additional supportive cues for differential diagnosis.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{A}}$ 

The summary should not contain any markdown, LaTeX, or citations.

There is no perfect clinical separation between COVID-19 and influenza; however, anosmia or dysgeusia occurs much more frequently in COVID-19 and serves as a key distinguishing feature. Combined with increased reports of gastrointestinal symptoms, these findings allow clinicians to make meaningful distinctions even without laboratory tests.
